zh.Aston Bruley, Worcestershire, England: 英國武斯特斯爾阿斯頓 索莫維爾
zh.lifeSummary: 當Richard de Bruley於大約1211年出生在英國英格蘭武斯特斯爾阿斯頓 馬尼亞時，他的父親Robert de Bruley27歲，母親Joan de Kinewarton23歲。他於1242年與Mrs. Millicent De Bruley在英國英格蘭武斯特斯爾伍斯特結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。他去世於1250年在英國武斯特斯爾阿斯頓 索莫維爾享年40歲。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Bruley
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Altered form of French Brûlé (see Brule ). This surname is also found in Britain, where it is of Huguenot origin.

variants.name: Brule
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Fernand, Lucien, Andre, Laurent, Marcel, Monique, Normand, Phillippe, Pierre, Raoul, Yvan, Yves.

topographic name for someone living in a place cleared for use by burning, from brûlé ‘burnt’ (Old French bruslé, past participle of brusler ‘to burn’). Some instances of the name may derive from the same word used with reference to disfigurement by burning, either accidentally or as a medieval ordeal or punishment.

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert

His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

en.lifeSummary: When Richard de Bruley was born about 1211, in Aston Magna, Worcestershire, England, United Kingdom, his father, Robert de Bruley, was 27 and his mother, Joan de Kinewarton, was 23. He married Mrs. Millicent De Bruley in 1242, in Worcester, Worcestershire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son. He died in 1250, in Aston Somerville, Worcestershire, England, at the age of 40.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Richard de Bruley est né environ 1211, à Aston Magna, Worcestershire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Robert de Bruley, avait 27 ans et sa mère, Joan de Kinewarton, avait 23 ans. Il a épousé Mrs. Millicent De Bruley en 1242, à Worcester, Worcestershire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Il est décédé en 1250, à Aston Somerville, Worcestershire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 40 ans.

Он женился на женщине по имени Mrs. Millicent De Bruley в 1242 г. в местности ворсестир, ворсестиршир, Англия, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Он умер в 1250 г. в местности астон сомирвилл, ворсестиршир, Англия в возрасте 40 лет.
